description: Replace MDX-based report writer with structured JSON output (Zod schema + generateObject), deterministic validator, and direct React renderer to eliminate dataRef coordination failures

userStories.title: Define Structured Report Zod Schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Zod schema defining the structured report format so that the writer can produce validated JSON output instead of free-form MDX.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: src/lib/ai/report-pipeline/report-schema.ts
Schema uses discriminated union for content blocks: prose, metrics, chart, callout, image
prose block: { type: 'prose', content: string } — content is markdown
metrics block: { type: 'metrics', items: MetricItem[] } — each item has title, value, tooltip (what/why/benchmark), optional colorClass and description
chart block: { type: 'chart', component: ChartComponent enum, dataRef: string } — component is one of the known chart/display component names
callout block: { type: 'callout', calloutType: 'tip' | 'warning' | 'note' | 'important', content: string }
image block: { type: 'image', src: string, alt: string, caption?: string } — for Python-generated charts
Section schema: { heading: string, blocks: ContentBlock[] }
Top-level report schema: { executiveSummary: string, sections: Section[], keyTakeaways: string[] }
All types exported alongside schemas (inferred via z.infer)

Component enum includes all chart components: EquityCurve, MonthlyChart, SymbolDistributionChart, DayOfWeekChart, HourHeatmap, SessionChart, RMultipleChart, MonteCarloChart, CalendarHeatmap, DrawdownTable, SymbolTable, DataTable
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Created src/lib/ai/report-pipeline/report-schema.ts with Zod discriminated union for 5 block types (prose, metrics, chart, callout, image), ChartComponent enum with all 12 components, and top-level structuredReportSchema. All types exported via z.infer.

userStories.title: Add aiGenerateObject Wrapper to AI Client
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an aiGenerateObject wrapper in the AI client module so that the writer phase can produce structured JSON output using the Vercel AI SDK generateObject function, with the same error handling as aiGenerateText.
acceptanceCriteria: Import generateObject from 'ai' in src/lib/ai/client.ts
New function aiGenerateObject<T> with options: model, system, messages, schema (Zod), maxRetries, temperature, maxOutputTokens
Returns { object: T, totalTokens: number } — the validated parsed object + token count
Uses same mapToOpenRouterError error handling as aiGenerateText
Generic type parameter T inferred from the Zod schema
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Added aiGenerateObject<T> wrapper to src/lib/ai/client.ts. Imports generateObject from 'ai' and z from 'zod'. Generic AiGenerateObjectOptions<T extends z.ZodType> with model, system, messages, schema, maxRetries, temperature, maxOutputTokens. Returns { object: z.infer<T>, totalTokens: number }. Uses same mapToOpenRouterError error handling. Required 'as z.infer<T>' cast on result.object due to complex SDK conditional types.

userStories.title: Enhance store_report_data with Component Type Metadata
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the store_report_data tool to accept an optional component field so that the gatherer can declare which chart component each dataset is intended for, enabling better validation and writer context.
acceptanceCriteria: Add optional component field to store_report_data input schema in src/lib/ai/tools/definitions.ts — z.string().optional() with description explaining it should be the component name
Update executeStoreReportData in src/lib/ai/tools/store-report-data.ts to store component metadata alongside data

Change dataStore type from Map<string, unknown> to Map<string, { data: unknown; component?: string; description: string }> — update the type in gatherer.ts interface, definitions.ts ToolContext, and generate-ai-report.ts
Update Object.fromEntries(dataStore) serialization in trigger task to only serialize the data field into dataArtifacts (keep DB schema unchanged)
Update gatherer system prompt to instruct the AI to always specify the component field when calling store_report_data
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Added DataStoreEntry interface and DataStoreMap type to report-schema.ts. Updated store_report_data tool with optional component field in input schema. Changed dataStore type from Map<string, unknown> to DataStoreMap across definitions.ts, index.ts, gatherer.ts, writer-context.ts, and generate-ai-report.ts. Updated executeStoreReportData to store { data, component?, description } entries. Updated trigger task serialization to extract only data field for DB persistence. Updated gatherer prompt to instruct AI to always specify component field. Updated buildDataSummary to show component type hints (e.g. equity-data -> EquityCurve).

userStories.title: Update Writer Context for Structured JSON Output
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the writer context builder to produce instructions optimized for JSON output instead of MDX, including available dataRef keys with component type hints from the gatherer metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/lib/ai/report-pipeline/writer-context.ts
Remove the MDX Catalog section (no longer needed — the Zod schema defines available components)
Remove MDX-specific formatting rules (LaTeX escaping, pipe tables, code fences)
Keep the Data Summary section — available dataRef keys with previews, aggregates, and sufficiency warnings
Enhance data summary to show component type hints from dataStore metadata: 'equity-data -> EquityCurve (88 rows)' instead of just 'equity-data (88 rows)'
Add JSON-specific formatting rules: markdown within prose blocks, no HTML tags in prose, cite specific numbers
Update buildWriterContext function signature to accept the enhanced dataStore type from US-003
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Rewrote writer-context.ts: removed MDX_CATALOG constant entirely (Zod schema now defines available components), replaced MDX-specific formatting rules with JSON-specific rules (markdown in prose blocks, no HTML tags, data citation, dataRef accuracy), kept Data Summary section with component type hints from US-003, exported COMPONENT_MINIMUMS for use by validator (US-007). Function signature unchanged (already accepts DataStoreMap from US-003).

userStories.title: Rewrite Writer Phase for Structured JSON Output
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the writer phase to use aiGenerateObject with the structured report Zod schema so that it produces validated JSON instead of raw MDX.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/lib/ai/report-pipeline/writer.ts
Use aiGenerateObject (from US-002) with the report schema (from US-001) instead of aiGenerateText
Update WriterResult to return { report: StructuredReport; tokensUsed: number } instead of { content: string; tokensUsed: number }
Rewrite WRITER_PERSONA and WRITER_INSTRUCTIONS for JSON output — instruct writer to produce structured JSON report, emphasize dataRef key accuracy, cite specific numbers
Include rules for when to skip chart blocks and use prose-only (insufficient data warnings)
Remove DATAREF_KEYS_PLACEHOLDER replacement logic — dataRef keys now part of writer context
Update WriterOptions to remove dataStoreKeys (now embedded in writerContext)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Rewrote writer.ts: replaced aiGenerateText with aiGenerateObject using structuredReportSchema. WriterResult now returns { report: StructuredReport, tokensUsed } instead of { content: string, tokensUsed }. Removed dataStoreKeys from WriterOptions (dataRef keys now embedded in writerContext from US-004). Removed DATAREF_KEYS_PLACEHOLDER replacement logic. Rewrote WRITER_PERSONA and WRITER_INSTRUCTIONS for JSON output with block type documentation, chart block rules, and skip-chart guidance. Updated trigger task minimally to pass writerContent as JSON.stringify(writerResult.report).

userStories.title: Update Few-Shot Examples for Structured JSON Format
userStories.description: As a developer, I want few-shot examples in JSON format so that the writer LLM learns to produce structured report output matching the Zod schema.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/lib/ai/report-pipeline/few-shot-examples.ts
Convert Performance Overview example from MDX to JSON section: heading, blocks array with prose, metrics, chart (EquityCurve), more prose, callout
Convert Risk Analysis example from MDX to JSON: multiple chart blocks, MetricGrid with 4 items, multiple callouts
Convert Sparse Data example from MDX to JSON: demonstrate skipping chart block and using prose + metrics instead
Examples must be valid JSON matching the report schema
Export as WRITER_FEW_SHOT_EXAMPLES string (same export name for compatibility)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Converted all three few-shot examples (Performance Overview, Risk Analysis, Sparse Data) from MDX to JSON format matching the StructuredReport Zod schema. Each example is now a section object with heading and blocks array using prose, metrics, chart, and callout block types. Updated WRITER_FEW_SHOT_EXAMPLES intro text to reference JSON format, block types, and section objects instead of MDX components. Export name unchanged for compatibility.

userStories.title: Create Deterministic Report Validator
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a fully deterministic validator that checks data integrity and injects prose fallbacks, replacing the current AI-powered repair loop, so that validation is predictable, faster, and cheaper.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/lib/ai/report-pipeline/validator.ts
Remove all AI repair logic (attemptRepair, REPAIR_SYSTEM_PROMPT, buildRepairPrompt)
Remove MDX compilation check (validateMdxCompilation, compileMDX import)
New runValidatorPhase accepts: { report: StructuredReport, dataStore: Map with enhanced type }
Returns: { report: StructuredReport, warnings: string[] } (always returns a valid report — fixes inline)
For each chart block: verify dataRef exists in dataStore — if missing, replace with prose fallback
For each chart block: verify data shape is compatible (array for most charts, { buckets, stats } for RMultipleChart, object for MonteCarloChart)
For each chart block: check data sufficiency using COMPONENT_MINIMUMS thresholds — if insufficient, replace with prose fallback
Collect warnings for any replacements made (logged but not blocking)
tokensUsed is always 0 (no LLM calls)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)

userStories.title: Build JSON Report Renderer Component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a React component that renders structured JSON reports directly (without MDX compilation) so that reports display reliably using the same chart components.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: src/components/report/report-renderer.tsx (client component)
ReportRenderer accepts: { report: StructuredReport, dataArtifacts: Record<string, unknown> }
Wraps content in ReportDataProvider (reuses existing provider from src/components/mdx/provider.tsx)
Renders executiveSummary as markdown via ReactMarkdown with remarkGfm
Renders each section with heading including id for TOC anchor linking
BlockRenderer handles prose (ReactMarkdown), metrics (MetricGrid + MetricCard), chart (existing chart wrappers), callout (Callout), image (ChartImage)
keyTakeaways renders as numbered list with markdown support per item
Styling matches current report output (same classes, spacing, Terminal design system)
All rendered elements include data-testid attributes
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
Verify in browser

Remove sanitizeMdxProse import and usage

If JSON parse fails, fall back to rendering raw content via ReactMarkdown

Keep TOC — extract headings from report.sections[].heading instead of regex on raw content
Keep active heading tracking with IntersectionObserver

userStories.title: Clean Up Deprecated MDX Infrastructure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove the now-unused MDX compilation infrastructure so that the codebase is clean and there's no confusion about which rendering path is active.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove src/lib/mdx/sanitize.ts (sanitizeMdxProse no longer needed)

If next-mdx-remote still needed elsewhere, keep it but remove report-specific compileMDX calls
Remove src/components/mdx/components.tsx (mdxComponents map) if only used by report rendering
Keep src/components/mdx/chart-wrappers.tsx — reused by JSON renderer
Keep src/components/mdx/provider.tsx — reused by JSON renderer
Keep src/components/mdx/data-table.tsx — reused by JSON renderer

userStories.title: Integration Tests for Structured Report Pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests for the new structured report pipeline so that we can verify schema validation, deterministic fallbacks, and rendering correctness.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: tests/integration/structured-report.test.ts
Test: report schema validates a well-formed report object (all block types)
Test: report schema rejects malformed input (missing required fields, invalid block types, invalid component names)
Test: deterministic validator passes a report with all valid dataRefs
Test: deterministic validator replaces chart block with prose when dataRef is missing from dataStore
Test: deterministic validator replaces chart block with prose when data shape is incompatible
Test: deterministic validator replaces chart block with prose when data is insufficient (below COMPONENT_MINIMUMS)
Test: executeStoreReportData stores component metadata correctly
All tests pass (bun run test)
